subject: Ecology
title: Pollution and Contamination
description: A quick, practical guide to Pollution and Contamination.

hook.text: Heavy metals, pesticides, and industrial waste can turn living soil into a toxic desert.

buildup.text: Lead from old paint and gasoline lingers in urban soils for decades after the source is gone.

discovery.text: Microplastics are now found in soils worldwide—fragments from tires, packaging, and textiles.

twist.text: Soil organisms ingest microplastics, and the chemicals leach into food webs we depend on.

climax.text: Bioremediation uses plants and microbes to clean contaminated soil—slowly but effectively.

punchline.text: Poison the soil and you poison the food chain.

quiz.question: What is bioremediation?
options: Using plants and microbes to clean contaminated soil
Removing all soil and replacing it
